meanings.usage: 指一种高速的小型船只
指突然改变方向的动作
控制车辆或船只方向的装置
描述极度的焦虑或绝望状态
指穿着滑雪板在水上滑行
标记水域位置或危险的漂浮物体
形容规模、程度或数量非常大
描述引擎或风等发出的响亮声音
指因失望或担忧而产生的情绪
指随风或水流缓慢移动
形容数量或程度较大但不是最大

examples.en: The driver swung the car round to avoid the obstacle.
examples.zh: 司机急转弯避开障碍物。

examples.en: The button came away from my coat when I caught it on the door.
examples.zh: 我衣服上的扣子被门刮掉了。

examples.en: The drowning man waved desperately to the people on the shore.
examples.zh: 溺水的人拼命向岸上的人挥手。

examples.en: The ship struck a buoy in the fog.
The airplane moved in a circle before landing.
examples.zh: 飞机在着陆前绕了一圈。

examples.en: A big wave came straight towards us.
examples.zh: 一个大浪直朝我们冲过来。

examples.en: The train roared past the station without stopping.
examples.zh: 火车轰鸣着驶过车站，没有停。

examples.en: They managed to swim out of danger before the boat returned.
examples.zh: 他们在船返回之前设法游出了危险区。

examples.en: The car slowed down as it approached the crossroads.
examples.zh: 汽车接近十字路口时放慢了速度。

examples.en: We've used up all the milk.

grammar.title: 过去完成时 (Past Perfect Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示在过去某一时刻之前已经完成的动作或状态
grammar.structure: 主语 + had + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于描述两个过去的动作，先发生的动作用过去完成时，后发生的动作用一般过去时

examples.en: Both men had hardly had time to realize what was happening when they were thrown into the sea.
examples.zh: 两个人还没来得及意识到发生了什么，就被猛地抛入海中。

examples.en: They had just begun to swim when they noticed the speedboat was moving in a circle.
examples.zh: 他们刚开始游泳，就注意到快艇正在绕圈移动。

grammar.title: had just enough time to
grammar.definition: 刚刚有足够的时间做某事
grammar.structure: had just enough time to + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 强调时间勉强足够，含有紧迫感

examples.en: They had just had enough time to swim out of danger when the boat again completed a circle.
examples.zh: 他们刚刚有时间游出危险区，快艇就又绕了一圈。

grammar.title: Before long
grammar.structure: Before long, + 主语 + 动词...
grammar.usage: 用于描述不久之后发生的事情，语气较正式

examples.en: Before long, the noise dropped completely and the boat began to drift gently.
examples.zh: 不久，声音完全消失，小船开始轻轻漂流。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Both + 主语 + had + 过去分词 + when + 过去时态句子

original.en: Both men had hardly had time to realize what was happening when they were thrown violently into the sea.
original.zh: 两个人几乎没时间意识到发生了什么，就被猛地抛入海中。

imitations.en: Both students had finished the exam when the bell rang.
imitations.zh: 铃声响起时，两个学生已经答完试卷了。

imitations.en: Both tourists had lost their way when it started to rain heavily.
imitations.zh: 天开始下大雨时，两个游客已经迷路了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + had just + 过去分词 + when + 过去时态句子

original.en: They had just had enough time to swim out of danger when the boat again completed a circle.
original.zh: 他们刚刚有时间游出危险区，快艇就又绕了一圈。

imitations.en: She had just closed the door when the telephone rang.
imitations.zh: 她刚关上门，电话就响了。

imitations.en: The children had just sat down when the teacher entered the classroom.
imitations.zh: 孩子们刚坐下，老师就进了教室。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Before long, + 主语 + 动词

original.en: Before long, the noise dropped completely and the boat began to drift gently across the water.
original.zh: 不久，声音完全消失，小船开始在水面上轻轻漂流。

imitations.en: Before long, the storm stopped and the sun came out.
imitations.zh: 不久，暴风雨停了，太阳出来了。

imitations.en: Before long, he realized his mistake.
imitations.zh: 不久，他就意识到了自己的错误。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + 动词 + in + 时间段

original.en: In less than a minute, it roared past them only a few feet away.
original.zh: 不到一分钟，它就在几英尺外呼啸而过。

imitations.en: In two hours, we reached the destination.
imitations.zh: 两个小时后，我们到达了目的地。

imitations.en: In a few seconds, the light went out.
